texto: Nothing succeeds like success; children who opt out of school have had a continued record of failure, and it would be difficult to blame the children themselves for voting with their feet and playing truant as much as possible. This failure is not necessary; it is imposed on the children by inappropriate methods of teaching which do not take into account the innate patterns of abilities of these children. A return to sanity is long overdue; we must pay close attention to the genetic basis of our children`s abilities.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hans Jürgen Eysenck: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Nothing succeeds like success; children who opt out of school have had a continued record of fail…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, education, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: what is new in his theories is not true, and what is true in his theories is not new.
explicacao: Citação de Hans Jürgen Eysenck sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “what is new in his theories is not true, and what is true in his theories is not new.” articula reflexao, freud, psychoanalysis em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como psychoanalysis e psychology.

texto: What you read in the newspapers, hear on the radio and see on television, is hardly even the truth as seen by experts; it is the wishful thinking of journalists, seen through filters of prejudice and ignorance.
explicacao: Citação de Hans Jürgen Eysenck sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “What you read in the newspapers, hear on the radio and see on television, is hardly even the trut…” articula reflexao, journalism, newspapers em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
